evidence.scientific_clarity: Table clearly shows 4 methods × 5 metrics with bold best-per-column and group labels (Baselines/Ours); claim is legible and well-structured.
evidence.typography: pdflatex produces embedded Type 1 CM fonts (10 fonts, all emb/sub/uni=yes); no DejaVu Sans; appropriate 9–10pt sizes; fully selectable text.
evidence.palette: Table format uses no color; colorblind safety is N/A; no directional/categorical color misuse.
evidence.layout_density: Compact single-page table with no gaping whitespace; booktabs rules provide clean separation; no overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly explains bold convention and reports 5 seeds; content matches rendered table; no legend needed for table format.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is genuine pdflatex vector output (PDF 1.7, 106 KB); pdffonts confirms 10 fully-embedded Type 1 fonts with selectable text.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; table.tex (required by brief) is absent from the output directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains bold convention and seed count (5 seeds) but contains no specific numerical finding or winning-method claim.

overall_strengths: Correct booktabs formatting with group spanning (Baselines/Ours) as specified in brief.
Proper bold-overlap logic implemented and pdflatex produces real vector PDF with fully embedded fonts.
overall_weaknesses: table.tex (the standalone LaTeX source required by the brief) is missing from the output directory.
Caption lacks a specific numerical claim or finding (e.g., which method wins and by how much).
